name: Condition Onset
definition: Descriptions of when and how a participant experienced onset of their chronic fatigue symptoms. [PDF counts: 19 files, 24 references]

name: Conditions
definition: Conditions described by participants. [PDF counts: 2 files, 2 references]

name: CFS
definition: Instances of participants describing themselves as having CFS. [PDF counts: 7 files, 7 references]

name: Congenital Condition
definition: Instances of participants describing themselves as having a congenital condition. [PDF counts: 1 files, 1 references]

name: EDS
definition: Instances of participants describing themselves as having EDS of any type. [PDF counts: 9 files, 10 references]

name: Fibromyalgia
definition: Instances of participants describing themselves as having Fibromyalgia. [PDF counts: 3 files, 3 references]

name: FND
definition: Instances of participants describing themselves as having FND. [PDF counts: 2 files, 2 references]

name: Hypermobility
definition: Instances of participants describing themselves as having JHS. [PDF counts: 11 files, 13 references]

name: Hypernoea
definition: Instances of participants describing themselves as having Hypernoea. Only 1 instance is recorded and information on the condition is not available. It is still included due to the participant description. [PDF counts: 1 files, 1 references]

name: IBS
definition: Instances of participants describing themselves as having IBS. [PDF counts: 1 files, 1 references]

name: Long Covid
definition: Instances of participants describing themselves as having Long Covid. [PDF counts: 2 files, 2 references]

name: ME
definition: Instances of participants describing themselves as having ME. [PDF counts: 1 files, 2 references]

name: MS
definition: Instances of participants describing themselves as having MS. [PDF counts: 1 files, 2 references]

name: PEM
definition: Instances of participants describing themselves as having PEM. [PDF counts: 4 files, 4 references]

name: POTS-Tachycardia
definition: Instances of participants describing themselves as having POTS or any type of Tachycardia. [PDF counts: 8 files, 10 references]

name: Sjogren's Syndrome
definition: Instances of participants describing themselves as having Sjogren’s Syndrome. [PDF counts: 1 files, 1 references]

name: Sleep Apnea
definition: Instances of participants describing themselves as having Sleep Apnea.

name: Small Fibre Neuropathy
definition: Instances of participants describing themselves as having Small Fibre Neuropathy. [PDF counts: 1 files, 1 references]

name: Device Use or Misuse
definition: Descriptions of tracker usage methods by participants.

name: Device Misuse
definition: Descriptions of the usage of trackers by participants in contrast to their designed intent.

name: Device Misuse > Negative Misuse
definition: Descriptions of the usage of trackers by participants in contrast to their designed intent that had negative results for the participant. [PDF counts: 1 files, 2 references]

name: Device Misuse > Positive Misuse
definition: Descriptions of the usage of trackers by participants in contrast to their designed intent that had positive results for the participant. [PDF counts: 13 files, 50 references]

name: Device Use
definition: Descriptions of the usage of trackers by participants within their intended and designed scope.

name: Device Use > Negative Use
definition: Descriptions of the usage of trackers by participants within their intended and designed scope that had negative results for the participant. [PDF counts: 4 files, 5 references]

name: Device Use > Positive Use
definition: Descriptions of the usage of trackers by participants within their intended and designed scope that had positive results for the participant. [PDF counts: 6 files, 16 references]

name: Devices and Apps
definition: Any reference to a tracker or a trackers features made by a participant.

name: Design Negative
definition: Negative feedback regarding some aspect of a tracker as described by a participant. [PDF counts: 1 files, 1 references]

name: Design Negative > Affordability
definition: Negative feedback from a participant describing the cost of trackers, whether initial purchase or some other cost such as subscriptions or replacement parts. [PDF counts: 4 files, 6 references]

name: Design Negative > Device Durability
definition: Negative feedback from a participant describing the physical durability of a tracker.

name: Design Negative > Energy Levels
definition: Negative feedback from a participant describing features of a tracker that record, calculate or present user energy levels at any point of use. [PDF counts: 3 files, 8 references]

name: Design Negative > Fitness Focus
definition: Negative feedback from a participant describing a tracker as having a focus on increasing the fitness of the user. [PDF counts: 11 files, 19 references]

name: Design Negative > Guilt
definition: Negative feedback from a participant describing any guilt felt by the participant as a result of tracker usage. [PDF counts: 5 files, 8 references]

name: Design Negative > Harassment
definition: Negative feedback from a participant describing instances they felt harassed by a tracker and its notifications. [PDF counts: 7 files, 12 references]

name: Design Negative > Physical Form
definition: Negative feedback from a participant describing the physical form of a tracker. [PDF counts: 2 files, 2 references]

name: Design Negative > Presentation
definition: Negative feedback from a participant describing the presentation of a tracker. This could be the basic user interface, or the detailed presentation of recorded data, or the lack of presentation of something the participants desired to see. [PDF counts: 5 files, 7 references]

name: Design Negative > Reading Frequency
definition: Negative feedback from a participant describing dissatisfaction regarding the frequency a tracker takes readings. For example, a participant with POTS may require more frequent heart rate readings. [PDF counts: 3 files, 7 references]

name: Design Negative > Skin Reactions
definition: Negative feedback from a participant describing reactions of the skin to the physical presence of a tracker, such as a watch strap. [PDF counts: 4 files, 6 references]

name: Design Negative > Sleep
definition: Negative feedback from a participant describing the sleep tracking features of a tracker. [PDF counts: 1 files, 1 references]

name: Design Negative > Steps
definition: Negative feedback from a participant describing the step counting and/or tracking features of a tracker. [PDF counts: 5 files, 14 references]

name: Design Negative > Symptom Record
definition: Negative feedback from a participant describing symptom recording features of a tracker, whether those features are automatically done by the tracker or are manually entered by the user. [PDF counts: 1 files, 1 references]

name: Design Negative > Workout Monitoring
definition: Negative feedback from a participant describing the workout monitoring features of a tracker. This can include the notifications given to the user when the tracker believes a workout is occurring, or notifications about workouts given to the user outside of these times (that isn’t more suitably coded as “Harassment” or “Guilt” instead). [PDF counts: 1 files, 2 references]

name: Design Suggestions
definition: Feedback from a participant suggesting some change in trackers. This is not restricted to just the tracker(s) the participant currently uses.

name: Design Suggestions > Include
definition: A suggestion from a participant to include some features or form factor.

name: Design Suggestions > Include > Form
definition: A suggestion from a participant regarding the physical form of a tracker. [PDF counts: 6 files, 7 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Include > Form > Ankle-Leg
definition: A suggestion from a participant of a preference for the physical form of a tracker located in the ankle or leg region. [PDF counts: 1 files, 1 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Include > Form > Arm
definition: A suggestion from a participant of a preference for the physical form of a tracker located in the arm region. [PDF counts: 2 files, 2 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Include > Form > Implant
definition: A suggestion from a participant of a preference for the physical form of a tracker as an implant. [PDF counts: 2 files, 5 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Include > Form > Necklace
definition: A suggestion from a participant of a preference for the physical form of a tracker as a necklace. [PDF counts: 1 files, 2 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Include > Form > Ring
definition: A suggestion from a participant of a preference for the physical form of a tracker as a ring. [PDF counts: 2 files, 2 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Include > Form > Wrist
definition: A suggestion from a participant of a preference for the physical form of a tracker located in the wrist region. [PDF counts: 8 files, 8 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Include > Function
definition: A suggestion from a participant regarding the function of a tracker. [PDF counts: 1 files, 1 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Include > Function > Alerts
definition: A suggestion from a participant regarding a trackers ability to use alerting features. [PDF counts: 6 files, 10 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Include > Function > App to Device
definition: A suggestion from a participant regarding a tracker comprising of a device Connection connecting to a mobile application. [PDF counts: 4 files, 4 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Include > Function > Blood Pressure
definition: A suggestion from a participant regarding a trackers ability to record blood pressure. [PDF counts: 5 files, 5 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Include > Function > Customisation
definition: A suggestion from a participant regarding a trackers ability to have it’s features, function, form, or some other aspect, customised. [PDF counts: 10 files, 16 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Include > Function > Focused Purpose
definition: A suggestion from a participant regarding a tracker possessing a focused design intent. [PDF counts: 2 files, 2 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Include > Function > Other Sensors
definition: A suggestion from a participant regarding a trackers possession of a sensor not described in other codes. [PDF counts: 5 files, 5 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Include > Function > Other Sensors > Respiration
definition: A suggestion from a participant regarding a trackers ability to track respiration metrics. [PDF counts: 3 files, 3 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Include > Function > Other Sensors > Symptom Tracking
definition: A suggestion from a participant regarding a trackers ability to track symptoms of the user, whether automatically or by manual entry from the user. [PDF counts: 5 files, 8 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Include > Function > Other Sensors > Sync To Other Devices
definition: A suggestion from a participant regarding a trackers ability to connect to and synchronise with other tracking or medical devices, such as insulin pumps. [PDF counts: 4 files, 5 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Include > Function > Other Sensors > Tailored Insight
definition: A suggestion from a participant regarding a trackers ability to offer insight tailored to the user. [PDF counts: 8 files, 15 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Include > Presentation
definition: A suggestion from a participant regarding the physical form of a tracker or its data and features.

name: Design Suggestions > Include > Presentation > Hardware
definition: A suggestion by a participant regarding changing the hardware makeup of a tracker. [PDF counts: 5 files, 7 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Include > Presentation > Software
definition: A suggestion by a participant regarding changing the software makeup of a tracker. [PDF counts: 14 files, 21 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Remove
definition: A suggestion from a participant to remove some features of a tracker.

name: Design Suggestions > Remove > Connectivity
definition: A suggestion from a participant to remove a trackers ability to connect to other devices, applications, or accounts. For example, the ability for a smart watch to connect to social media or music players. [PDF counts: 2 files, 2 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Remove > Connectivity > Mobile Apps
definition: A suggestion from a participant to remove a trackers ability to connect to applications on a mobile phone. [PDF counts: 1 files, 1 references]

name: Design Suggestions > Remove > Connectivity > Monetisation
definition: A suggestion from a participant to remove a trackers monetisation, such as a subscription requirement for the full suite of features. [PDF counts: 1 files, 1 references]

name: Device Adoption
definition: A description or reference by a participant to the adoption of a tracker. This could include why the tracker was adopted, when, or how it was adopted. [PDF counts: 13 files, 17 references]

name: Device Positive
definition: Positive feedback regarding some aspect of a tracker as described by a participant. [PDF counts: 1 files, 1 references]

name: Device Positive > Affordability
definition: Positive feedback from a participant describing the affordability of a tracker. This could be regarding a subscription, the initial cost of purchase, or the cost of repair. [PDF counts: 2 files, 2 references]

name: Device Positive > Alerts
definition: Positive feedback from a participant describing the trackers ability alert the user to some change in recorded metrics. [PDF counts: 7 files, 10 references]

name: Device Positive > Convenience
definition: Positive feedback from a participant describing the convenience of a trackers, whether that’s convenience of use or convenience of form. [PDF counts: 9 files, 14 references]

name: Device Positive > Customisation
definition: Positive feedback from a participant describing the customisation features of a tracker. [PDF counts: 2 files, 4 references]

name: Device Positive > Energy Levels
definition: Positive feedback from a participant describing the ability for a tracker to record, derive, or present energy levels to the user. [PDF counts: 9 files, 17 references]

name: Device Positive > Form
definition: Positive feedback from a participant describing the physical form of the tracker. [PDF counts: 2 files, 3 references]

name: Device Positive > Heart Rate
definition: Positive feedback from a participant describing the ability for the tracker to record heart rate or associated measurements (such as HRV). [PDF counts: 14 files, 48 references]

name: Device Positive > Pacing
definition: Positive feedback from a participant describing the ability for the participant to pace as a result of using the tracker. [PDF counts: 15 files, 45 references]

name: Device Positive > Respiration
definition: Positive feedback from a participant describing the ability for a tracker to record respiration. [PDF counts: 1 files, 1 references]

name: Device Positive > Sleep
definition: Positive feedback from a participant describing the sleep tracking features of a tracker. [PDF counts: 9 files, 12 references]

name: Device Positive > Smartphone Linking
definition: Positive feedback from a participant describing the ability for a tracker to link with a smartphone. [PDF counts: 3 files, 3 references]

name: Device Positive > Steps
definition: Positive feedback from a participant describing the step counting or step tracking features of a tracker. [PDF counts: 7 files, 24 references]

name: Device Positive > Workout Monitoring
definition: Positive feedback from a participant describing the workout monitoring features of a tracker. This can include the notifications given to the user when the tracker believes a workout is occurring, or notifications about workouts given to the user outside of these times (that isn’t more suitably coded as “Alerts”, “Pacing”, or some other code instead). [PDF counts: 10 files, 23 references]

name: Device Types
definition: Classification of the types of tracker a participant describes using. [PDF counts: 1 files, 3 references]

name: Device Types > Application
definition: Classification of a tracker that a participant discusses as an application. [PDF counts: 12 files, 27 references]

name: Device Types > Chest Device
definition: Classification of a tracker that a participant discusses as a chest device, such as a heart rate monitoring strap. [PDF counts: 1 files, 1 references]

name: Device Types > Cycling App
definition: Classification of a tracker that a participant discusses as a cycling application, eg. Zwift. [PDF counts: 1 files, 2 references]

name: Device Types > Gamified Devices
definition: Classification of a tracker that a participant discusses as a gamified tracker. [PDF counts: 2 files, 3 references]

name: Device Types > Swimming Device
definition: Classification of a tracker that a participant discusses as a swimming tracker. [PDF counts: 1 files, 1 references]

name: Device Types > Wrist Device
definition: Classification of a tracker that a participant discusses as a wrist tracker. [PDF counts: 15 files, 22 references]

name: Usage
definition: Descriptions from a participant regarding when and how they use the tracker.

name: Usage > Live Monitoring
definition: Description from a participant of using a tracker to monitor measurements live. [PDF counts: 13 files, 25 references]

name: Usage > Proof of Symptoms
definition: Description from a participant of using a tracker to confirm the presence and/or effect of symptoms. [PDF counts: 12 files, 38 references]

name: Usage > Reflective Monitoring
definition: Description from a participant of using a tracker to monitor measurements after they happen, in a reflective manner. For example, looking back on the days measurements in the evening. [PDF counts: 17 files, 67 references]

name: Positive Activities
definition: Descriptions of activities that a participant describes as positive. This could be something the participant can still partake in despite their condition, or something the participants partakes in to ease symptoms.

name: Exercise
definition: Descriptions of activities that a participant describes as positive, such as yoga. [PDF counts: 3 files, 4 references]

name: Recreation
definition: Descriptions of activities that a participant describes as positive, including hobbies. [PDF counts: 2 files, 3 references]

name: Social
definition: Descriptions of activities that a participant describes as positive with a social purpose.

name: Work and Housework
definition: Descriptions of activities that a participant describes as positive involving some form of employed work or work to maintain a participants surroundings, such as cleaning.

name: Prevented Activities
definition: An activity the participants describes as being prevented from participation due to their conditions and/or symptoms.

name: Everything
definition: A description of general interference or non-function from the participant. [PDF counts: 11 files, 14 references]

name: Hobbies
definition: A described instance of the participant being prevented from taking part in hobbies.

name: Hobbies > Exercise
definition: A described instance of the participant being prevented from taking part in a form of exercise. For example, going to the gym to workout. [PDF counts: 7 files, 15 references]

name: Hobbies > Recreation
definition: A described instance of the participant being prevented from taking part in a form of recreation. For example, walks along the beach. [PDF counts: 3 files, 9 references]

name: Hobbies > Sport
definition: A described instance of the participant being prevented from taking part in sport. [PDF counts: 3 files, 5 references]

name: Housework
definition: A described instance of the participant being prevented from being able to complete tasks to maintain the standard of their home. [PDF counts: 7 files, 10 references]

name: Prevented Activities > Social
definition: A described instance of the participant being prevented from taking part in social activities, such as meeting friends. [PDF counts: 10 files, 15 references]

name: Travel
definition: A described instance of the participant being prevented from taking travelling to a location outside of their home. [PDF counts: 6 files, 12 references]

name: Work
definition: A described instance of the participant being prevented from completing employed work. [PDF counts: 5 files, 6 references]

name: Symptoms
definition: A description or reference to a symptom of the participant.

name: Cognitive
definition: A description or reference to a cognitive symptom of the participant. [PDF counts: 1 files, 1 references]

name: Cognitive > Brain Fog
definition: A description or reference to brain fog by the participant. [PDF counts: 19 files, 41 references]

name: Cognitive > Memory Problems
definition: A description or reference to memory loss by the participant. [PDF counts: 12 files, 18 references]

name: Cognitive > Proprioception
definition: A description or reference to proprioception (the ability of a person to be aware of where their body is in the surrounding space) by the participant. [PDF counts: 2 files, 2 references]

name: Cognitive > Speech
definition: A description or reference to a participants speech being affected by a symptom. [PDF counts: 6 files, 10 references]

name: General Fatigue
definition: A description or reference to the symptom of general fatigue, both mental and physical, by the participant. [PDF counts: 16 files, 28 references]

name: Physical
definition: A description or reference to a physical symptom of the participant. [PDF counts: 2 files, 3 references]

name: Physical > Bowel-Bladder Movements
definition: A description or reference to bowel or bladder movement problems by the participant. [PDF counts: 2 files, 2 references]

name: Physical > Dizziness
definition: A description or reference to dizziness by the participant. [PDF counts: 7 files, 9 references]

name: Physical > Fatigue
definition: A description or reference to physical fatigue by the participant. [PDF counts: 8 files, 15 references]

name: Physical > Headache-Migraine
definition: A description or reference to headaches or migraines by the participant. [PDF counts: 1 files, 1 references]

name: Physical > Hearing Problems
definition: A description or reference to hearing loss or interference by the participant. [PDF counts: 2 files, 2 references]

name: Physical > Heart Rate-Blood Pressure
definition: A description or reference to abnormal heart rate or blood pressure by the participant. [PDF counts: 8 files, 12 references]

name: Physical > Muscle Problems
definition: A description or reference to muscular problems, such as spasms or stiffness, by the participant. [PDF counts: 9 files, 10 references]

name: Physical > Nausea
definition: A description or reference to nausea by the participant. [PDF counts: 2 files, 3 references]

name: Physical > Nerve Issues
definition: A description or reference to nerve issues, such as a loss of feeling, or an extreme amount of a feeling, by the participant. [PDF counts: 5 files, 8 references]

name: Physical > Pain
definition: A description or reference to experiencing physical pain by the participant. [PDF counts: 17 files, 40 references]

name: Physical > PEM
definition: A description or reference to post-exertional malaise or some similar symptoms by the participant. [PDF counts: 14 files, 24 references]

name: Physical > Respiration
definition: A description or reference to problems with respirations by the participant. [PDF counts: 5 files, 6 references]

name: Physical > Sleep Problems
definition: A description or reference to a lack of or an inconsistency in sleeping by the participant. [PDF counts: 18 files, 32 references]

name: Physical > Struggle to Stand or Walk etc
definition: A description or reference to struggling to stand, walk, sit, or similar, by the participant. [PDF counts: 16 files, 21 references]

name: Physical > Vision Problems
definition: A description or reference to a lack of or interference of vision by the participant. [PDF counts: 4 files, 4 references]
